mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS path lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so a declining policy rate predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is confirmed when rate cuts are followed by broad index gains and fails when equity returns do not improve after easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in unemployment signals weakening household income and higher recession risk, which depresses earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following month. The effect should appear only after the labor market deterioration is visible in the data.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 45% of cases, or if the signal loses negative Rank IC significance in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in real activity and household demand, measured by rising INDPRO and RSAFS, indicates expanding nominal sales and output, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is strongest when both series trend upward together rather than separately.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive changes in INDPRO and RSAFS fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns above baseline, or if the interaction term is not materially better than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production reflects weakening goods-sector activity and margin pressure, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal is a short-horizon risk-off indicator and should be visible when output momentum turns down.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month declines in INDPRO are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if the effect reverses in a rolling 2-year test.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is falling while FEDFUNDS remains very low, real policy is easy and financial conditions loosen, supporting equity prices over the next month. The mechanism fails if lower inflation is accompanied by tightening rates or if disinflation reflects demand collapse rather than benign easing.
failure_condition: Reject if low-rate disinflation periods do not show above-baseline 20-day SPX gains, or if positive returns disappear once CPIAUCSL declines are controlled for with FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in housing starts signals stronger construction demand, wealth effects, and downstream consumption, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is confirmed when sustained housing acceleration is followed by broad equity strength.
failure_condition: Reject if housing-starts rebounds do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the predictive relation vanishes after accounting for the baseline market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE marks a genuine labor-market deterioration that depresses earnings expectations and increases recession pricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The interaction is necessary because either series alone can be noisy.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls and UNRATE rises over the same month
failure_condition: Reject if months with falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE do not underperform on 20-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal is not stronger than either labor series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary or post-shock rebound regime, a stronger-than-expected rise in GDPC1 marks an improving earnings backdrop and should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The signal is regime-conditional because GDP strength matters most when the economy is emerging from weakness.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP upturns in post-shock periods do not lead to positive 60-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the sign is not better than random outside recovery windows.
